name: Bean Stew with Fennel and Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 25 minutes
Soaking Time: several hours
Cooking Time: 1 hour
1. Soak the beans in a large bowl of water to cover for several hours or overnight. Drain them.
2. Put the soaked beans into a pot with stock or broth and enough water to cover by 2 inches. Add bay leaf and whole garlic cloves (crushed). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, until beans are tender (about 45 minutes). The beans should be softer than al dente but not cooked to the point where they begin to disintegrate. Do not drain the beans until after adding fennel and tomato sauce.
3. Meanwhile, remove stalks from the fennel bulb. Chop the feathery leaves and set aside. Trim off the root end of the bulbs; cut them in half lengthwise and slice thinly. There should be about 8 cups of sliced fennel. Discard the stalks or save for soup. Heat 1/3 cup olive oil in a large heavy skillet; cook the fennel over medium-low heat until wilted and translucent, about 10-12 minutes. Do this in batches if necessary.
4. Add tomato to the fennel. Remove garlic and bay leaf from the beans. Drain the beans. Stir them into the skillet, gently tossing to combine all ingredients. Add parsley and chopped fennel leaves. Season with salt and pepper. Remove from heat, add lemon juice, and drizzle remaining olive oil to taste. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: 6 to 8 Servings
